tickets.description:
Ticket 4015 requested cancel GC pool service.  Owner continued to be bill. Comp 472.50. Send ticket today to Edgar to stop billing. Chelsea confirmed she has not been billed or billed GC for service.

Hello Dave and Zach

This is now an ongoing issue that I am being charged for Pool services that I cancelled on Jan 13th 2026
I just reviewed my statement, and I was charged another $135 for Splash Pros Pool - which I did not hire.  This is May's statement but says April's charge
So now I have been charged for
Jan Prime Pool $135,
Feb Prime Pool $135 
March Splash Pros Pool $135
May Splash Pros Pool $135 (States April)

That's a total of $540.00.  I informed you on Jan 13 to cancel Prime Pools and they never came again, so I should only have to pay for 2 sessions at $67.50
I also informed you I have hired my own pool maintenance.  As discussed in all of the emails.  I was expecting a refund on my statement
This is what gulf coast owes me.  Can you please take care of this.
$540.00 - $67.50 = $472.50

tickets.subject: Res. No. 53109 - Guest:  Victoria Metker - Golden Beach #1:     Issue: 2 of 2:  "sinking" mattress
tickets.description:
Hi Sandra!

Guest Victoria Metker, stay dates:  May 25 - May 30, 2026 - she noted the following issue:

Issue 2 of 2:  "sinking" mattress

Sandra, I know that mattress comfort is very subjective, but this guest says the mattress is "sinking" - could you perhaps have someone take a look at the mattress?  Perhaps it is in need of replacment?   Thank you!

The guest's review was as follows:

Location is perfect but condo needs some TLC.  The location of the condo is perfect being beachfront with fantastic views. However the interior of condo needs some TLC with a few repairs/updates needed here and there such as wall paint scuffs, front door seal, sinking mattress etc to name a few. Also all four of the Golden Beach condos seem to have an insect problem. Ours in Unit 1 definately had it and our neighbor in 2 said they had the same issue so its obviously a whole building issue versus individual unit issue. (Been visiting Sanibel for years and never encountered that before in other rentals) Having said that, the actual 'cleaning' of the condo was great, spotless actually, so the cleaning staff are doing the best they can with what they are given. The few incidental entry issues we had as far as Garage Door code,wifi password etc etc were handled super efficiently by Peyton at Sanibel Island Vacations so no complaints there. With improvements this condo could be spectacular - however as is I probably wouldnt rent again.


Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Todd Golden/Realtor/Property Management Inquiry
tickets.description:
Hello,
I have a client considering purchasing a unit at Gulf & Bay Club Bayside and would like to learn more about your rental management program for owners.
Could you please provide information regarding:
  • Your current rental management fee structure and commission rates
  • Any additional fees charged to owners
  • Services included in the management program
  • Occupancy rates and average rental performance, if available
  • Any owner restrictions or requirements related to participating in the rental program
I would appreciate any information or materials you can share.
Thank you for your time, and I look forward to hearing from you.
Best regards,


Todd Golden

Cleanliness  Issues from reviews:    
a)  Dirty/dusty;
b)  Stains;  (same complaint from 2 x review)
c)  Dirty bathroom;  (same complaint from 2 x reviews)
d)  Clogged shower and bathrrom sink;
e)  The bedsheets and blankets were not clean ( 2 x reviews); towels looked like they had faeces stains on 
them.

Would you be able to take a look at these issues?  Would you possibly be able to let me have photos as evidence that this has been attended to, so I can use it at evidence, if needed?  

Many thanks!

Kind Regards,

tickets.subject: Knapp Colleen/Beach House Residences Unit 1/Res #: 39681/Dishwasher - Soap Dispenser
tickets.description:
Date and Time Reported:  June 8, 9pm
Date of Reservation: CI: 06/06/2026 - CO: 06/11/2026
Concerns:  Guest reports dishwasher is not properly operating. Soap dispenser is not opening and detergent pods are not fully dissolving, resulting in dishes not being cleaned.

Actions Taken:  Provided troubleshooting steps to the guest but still not working properly. Results of the troubleshooting are below,

Guest Notes / Observations:

  • Soap dispenser is not opening during cycles.
  • Breaker was flipped/reset and unit was restarted, but issue persists.
  • Dishwasher has been run 3 times with no improvement.
  • When dispenser was manually left open, pod still only partially dissolved.

Wash Performance Details:

  1. Food residue remains on dishes after cycle.
  2. Detergent pod does not fully dissolve unless dispenser is manually opened; even then, only partially dissolves.
  3. Guest believes water is running (unit sounds operational and dishes are wet after cycle).
  4. Unsure if spray arms are functioning properly.
  5. No standing water remains in the bottom after cycle.

Additional Notes:

  • Unit appears to be filling/running water, but cleaning performance is poor.
  • Potential issue with detergent dispenser mechanism, water pressure, or spray arm circulation.

Requested Action:
Please inspect dishwasher operation, focusing on:

  • Soap dispenser mechanism
  • Water spray circulation / spray arms
  • Detergent dissolution during cycle
  • Overall wash performance system check

tickets.subject: Possible pool leak at Dreams of Paradise
tickets.description:
Dave,
I spoke with the guest that is staying at Dreams of Paradise right now. When they arrived, the pool was dingy so we sent the pool vendor out.  The problem was the water was below the pump line so the pump wasn't working.  They filled the pool and now it's fine.  She said that they have to fill it 2-3 inches each day.   Please alert the owner that they might have a leak.  Please let me know if the owner wants to look into this or if they want us to send someone to look at it,

The place is beautiful and provided us with lots of space! The pool is kept very clean and the backyard is nice as well as having activities to do at the house! In the backyard two of the fans did not work when we tried to turn them on and the master bedroom gets pretty hot during the night. I will also say there was no stand up mirrors in the home.



Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Res. No. - Guest:  James Hill  - Sandy Pointe     Issue:  2 of 2:  Ice maker not working
tickets.description:
Hi John!

Guest James Hill,  stay dates: April 28 - May 14, 2026 - he noted the following issue:

Ice maker in fridge not working

John, would you posisbly be able to have a technician takea look at the ice maker in the fridge?  Guest said it's not working. 

This guest, James Hill, has stated that he is trturning for another visit at the end of July and it would be great if we could have that ice maker sorted (I am certain it will be attended to longe before that, though! LOL) 

He also stated that he "would like to have a different mattress in the master bedroom." - I have rasied a ticekt to Dave for that, just so he is aware.

Here is his next reservation:

The guest's review was as follows:

A Relaxing Stay.  The house was clean and well maintained. I liked being able to park my car in the garage out of the sun. I enjoyed the pool. However, I called the day of my arrival to make sure that the pool heater was working and it was not. I was there about 48 hours before the temperature of the pool was at a comfortable level for me. I am supposed to receive a refund for two days cost of heating the pool. The ice maker in the refrigerator did not work. The mattress in the master bedroom was too soft for me and did not support my back. It is a foam mattress with no box springs. It should be replaced with a traditional mattress and box springs. The neighborhood was quiet. I am returning for another stay at the end of July but I would like to have a different mattress in the master bedroom. The other beds also have the same kind of mattress.

Note:  The pool issue was taken care of on ticket# 6093 and the ticket to dave for the mattress is #6914.

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: VRBO Health and Safety email
tickets.description:
Good morning, team. For your kind assistance pls


Dear Gulf Coast Vacation Rentals,

Property: Spacious 3/2 waterfront pool home in Port Charlotte - Scenic Solitude (99424879)

Vrbo Listing Number: 9877226

Expedia Group Health & Safety Case No: 153096069

Expedia Group has not yet received a response regarding potential health and safety concerns at this Vrbo property.

For reference, a Vrbo traveler who stayed at this property reported experiencing:

" Pool was dirty and low on water. Baking dishes needed to be cleaned prior to use. Furniture pillows and mattress cover stained. Cobwebs, dust, exposed nails, holes and scuffed walls."
"Ants in the bathroom along with cockroaches of which six were caught in our actual overnight stay of 10 nights"

To help ensure the safety of future travelers, please submit your responses to the following questions regarding this property's health and safety features and practices by June 15th, 2026.

Additionally, please include details for any actions already taken or plans to address the reported concerns:

Is the pool water clear with the bottom of the pool visible (at its deepest point) at all times?
Is the pool(s) regularly cleaned (including the grease line)?
Is the water tested as per local regulations for pH balance and recorded in a logbook?
Is there a documented chemical disinfectant treatment system in place?
Are pool chemical dosing records retained?
The records should as a minimum be signed by the pool maintenance person and include date, times the chemicals
used and the dosage. Where more than one pool is a feature of the property, individual pool records must be
retained and be clearly labelled with the pool name. Is this the case?

Is there a process in place if the property is not up to guest standards?
If a cleaning company is used, how do they report to the owner/manager of issues found?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?
Do you have a cleaning schedule indicating which areas are cleaned, frequency of cleaning, chemicals to be used and any special protective equipment required?

Is a program of pest control measures in place?
Does this cover food control areas, public areas & bedrooms/apartments?
Please provide the name of your contracted pest control company
What is the date of the last inspection?
Did the Pest Control company find evidence of cockroach infestation?
If yes, what action was taken?
Were the surrounding rooms also treated? ¯
How often are rooms cleaned?
Is a thorough inspection of mattresses/furniture etc. carried out?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?

In the interest of safety, if we do not hear from you, we may be required to temporarily suspend this property on our sites until we receive your response.

Your professionalism and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. Please reply to this email if you have any questions.

Best Regards,

Raquel

Expedia Group Health & Safety Operations

healthandsafety@expedia.com

tickets.subject: Res #: 52418 | Rising Tide | Groesser Ty | HVAC ISSUE
tickets.description:
Issue Reported:
Guest reports that the AC is struggling to maintain the set temperature. Thermostat is set to 74°F, but the home is remaining around 79°F.

Guest Feedback:
• AC has been running continuously rather than cycling on and off.
• Air coming from the vents feels only slightly cool, not cold.
• Guest has young children and is concerned about comfort, particularly during sleeping hours.

Contacted Hometown Air and WO created as well. Provided the guest phone number so AC guy can contact them directly.

tickets.subject: Res. No. 45454 - Guest:  Kathy Tripp; Gulf & Bay 1274:     Issue: 2 of 2:  Front & master bedroom door issues
tickets.description:
Hi John! 

Guest Kathy Tripp, stay dates: May 31 - June 07, 2026 - she noted the following issues (in her private comment, not in her actual review):

Issue 2 of 2:  The front entrance door & the master bedroom doors do not close properly unless you pull on them.

Please could you have someone take a look to see if they can fix these doors?  Thank you!

The guest's private comments were as follows:

There were stains on the carpet when we arrived and was asked by your staff if someone could come and clean it. Two days no one showed up so we told them not to come until after we left.
Also the entrance door and master bedroom door did not close without pulling on it. Should be fixed. Did not expect condiments but salt and pepper would have been nice.

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Knapp Colleen | Res #: 39681 | Dryer Not Drying Properly – Flow Sense Error (d90)
tickets.description: Guest reported that the dryer is not drying towels properly. They initially received a "clean filter" warning and cleaned the lint filter, but the issue persisted through multiple cycles. Upon further inspection, they noticed the Flow Sense indicator is illuminated and the dryer is displaying a d90 error code, suggesting a possible airflow or vent blockage issue. The guest is requesting for someone to come in to check the situation. Thank you!

tickets.subject: Sandhya | Possible Lead
tickets.description:
Hello Team, kindly assist potential guest. 

Sandhya message us via Airbnb OTA: "We’re looking for a 2 bedroom/2 bath property.  While we prefer townhomes or villas, we are open to condos that are first floor or have an elevator.  We were first looking at areas with some walkability like  downtown but since it’s our first time in Sarasota, we were a little nervous about the safety of some of the areas.  We chose the Meadows condo because we have a friend who owns there and thought it would be a safe choice for first timers.  We don’t need a swimming pool but a lush backyard is very appealing.  We also need a washer/ dryer in the unit.  Hope this helps.
Sandhya"

tickets.subject: Res. No. 32405 - Guest:  Kenneth Allport - Sandalfoot 5B2:     Issue:  3 of 3:  Beach Equipment
tickets.description:
Hi Lais,

Guest Kenneth Allport; stay dates: May 9 - May 16,2026 - he noted the following issues:

Issue 3 of 3:  Beach equipment

Guest reported thatt he beach equipment was in poor condition.  Please could you have someone take a look at it to see what needs to be replaced?   The items the guest mentioned were as follows:

3a)  Chairs have mold spots on them;
3b)  Chairs hard to open; and
3c)  umbrella was broken had to purchase one to get thru the week.  

The guest's review was as follows:

Our stay was pretty good a few issues need some attention. Condo itself was pretty good. Bed on the other hand was probably the worst we ever slept in the 22 years we been coming here. Also the closet door in the bedroom kept coming off track every time you opened it. Beach equipment was also the worst I used. Chairs have mold spots on them and hard to open, umbrella was broken had to purchase one to get thru the week. Obviously very old equipment. Stayed last year in different unit where the beach equipment was new. We really like coming here every year but most likely stay in other unit next time.

Thank you!

tickets.subject: Res. No. 50451 - Guest:  Cynthia Lemke - Florida Oasis:     Issue:  Safety concern:  tile around pool very slippery
tickets.description:
Hi Dave! 

Guest Cynthia Lemke; stay dates: May 27 - June 3,2026 - noted the following issue:

Ceramic tiles around pool are extremely slippery

(this is possibly a safety issue?)  I looked at the photos in DW to see if they have any "Pool Rules" posted up anywhere, as the guest was concerned about the slipperiness. 

The guest's review was as follows:

"Our stay was very enjoyable! We appreciated all the amenities that was available to us in the house. The sand toys,chairs and umbrella made our visit to the beach easier! The kid toy bikes kept my grandson very occupied. The only concern I had was the ceramic tile around the pool (which was absolutely beautiful) became extremely slippery when wet! We had to lay beach towels down around the pool to prevent any falls. Other than that it was a perfect vacation and would definitely book again."

I'm not sure how it works over there in Florida, but if someone slips and gets hurt, what happens?  Do the guests need to sign an indemnity form or anything?

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Res. No. 41085 - Guest  Kelly Haddon - A Wave from it All     Issue:  Back porch furniture seems to have dry rot
tickets.description:
Hi Sandra,

Guest Kelly Haddon; 24 May - 30 May 2026, - noted the following issues:

1.  Check-in / access issues (missing key, incorrect gate code) - these issues have been attended  to and resolved on ticket# 6597;

2.  (this ticket):  Furnture on back porch seems to have dry rot - guest said it felt like the chairs were going to collapse.

Sandra, would you be able to send someone to take a look at this furniture?  I am not sure if this would require the furniture to be repaired or replaced?

The guest's review was as follows:

Trip to Anna Maria.  Overall experience was pretty good, had a little issue when checking in due to a missing key and incorrect gate code however my dinner was reimbursed for the issue, thank you . Also due to the gate code not working our second vehicle in our group had to come and go with me ,we couldn’t split up. This was very inconvenient because each unit only has one gate fob and I have an infant that needs oxygen therapy at times and can’t handle the sun as long as others in our group. Lastly furniture on back porch was very dry rotted on the seats and felt like they are about to give out at anytime. Some positives are the unit was stocked very nicely and a beautiful place to stay.

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Finklea Wendy | Res #: 25475 | Signal Inn 19 | Feedback from previous guest
tickets.description:
Hi Team, guest sent this text providing feedback with the unit. For your kind assistance please

1. The two downstairs bedrooms have mattresses that are old, sagging and need replacement as soon as possible. They don't need to be expensive mattresses, but fresh.
2. The toaster is missing a handle, making it impossible to use the left side. I have provided a photo.
3. The draining rack is the same one. We use 30 years ago. It is old, discolored, rested and needs to be replaced. Again, not an expensive fix photo provided.
4. The garbage can in the kitchen will not stay closed without extra efforts and opens periodically on its own while working in the kitchen. Not only is this a safety hazard, but can be remedied easily with a strong magnet attached or a latch to help keep it closed. No need for replacement, just make an effort to let it be secured. This has been an issue with this garbage can for at least 10 or 15 years! I have provided a photo.
5. The master bathroom has a large ceiling crack that needs to be fixed. I have provided a photo.We hope to continue our family gathering tradition at Signal Inn, however it's time to fix some of these long overdue issues. Your fees have gone up exponentially, so should the quality of the stay. Thank you. Wendy Finklea

tickets.subject: test 2665
tickets.description:
Guest is having issues with:

Trouble shooting Actions Taken (delete actions not applicable)

  • What kind of thermostat is in the unit? Is it a standard or a smart thermostat?  The most common cause of an AC not working is that the breaker has "tripped" and needs to be reset.  Ask the guest if they are comfortable locating and checking the breaker.   If not, we can send someone out to check it for them, but it might take longer.

  • If Smart thermostat:

    • If it is not changing the temperature when you adjust it, then it is likely only able to be changed by the homeowner and we will need to reach out to the homeowner.   

    • To operate a smart thermostat, you simply need to touch the screen then move your finger around to adjust to the desired temperature.  

    • Make sure the smart thermostat is connected to the Wi-Fi network.

  • If standard thermostat:

    • what temperature do you have it set on? (If the temp is too low, there is a chance they may freeze the system and they need to understand that an air conditioner can handle temperatures 20 degrees cooler than the outside air, so in the summer 75 is doable for an AC)

    • Standard thermostats require batteries.  If there is nothing displayed on the screen, the battery may be dead.  To change the batteries, the guest can simply pop off the front cover and insert new batteries. 

    • Sometimes the thermostat can trip the breaker and the guest simply needs to check the breaker box and flip the switch back to on.

    • Is the air kicking on and running at all? (Sometimes the system may just need to be turned off and back on. If the air is coming on but no cool air (or warm air for heat) is coming out, then there may be an issue) and a vendor will need to be sent to the home.

Reference:  AC/HEAT Thermostat Trouble Shooting – Gulf Coast Property Management (zendesk.com)

Additional Notes:

tickets.subject: Unit: 7th Heaven | Guest Name: Drawbaugh Jackson | Smoke Detector Beeping
tickets.description:
Hi Team,

Guest called to report that the smoke detector in the main bedroom was beeping intermittently. The guest checked the detector and removed the battery to stop the beeping. Guest stated they were unable to find a replacement battery and were unsure whether the detector needed to remain operational during their stay.

Additional Details:
  • Smoke detector with issue is located in the main bedroom.
  • Guest removed the battery and will leave it on the dresser for housekeeping/maintenance to locate.
  • Guest advised that the other smoke detectors in the home appear to be functioning properly.
  • Guest is comfortable waiting until after departure for the issue to be addressed and does not require immediate service during the stay.
Thank you!

tickets.description:
Hi Zach,

I wanted to escalate a guest matter to you. The guest checking in on June 11 made their reservation on May 8. They explained that they were selling their home, but the buyer backed out one day before closing. They are now requesting either a refund or to move their reservation at least two months out to give them time to find another buyer.
I’ve held to the property’s terms and conditions, but the guest asked to escalate and speak with someone higher. I endorsed this to Carlos last Friday, but I believe he didn't have time yet to call the guest. I also messaged Beth, who mentioned she was out this morning and advised me to escalate this matter with you.

Would you be able to call the Guest Zach? or Please advise how you’d like us to proceed Thank you

tickets.subject: Health and Safety Email
tickets.description:
Hi Leeanne, we were advise to create a ticket for this kind of email. For your assistance please 

Dear Gulf Coast Vacation Rentals, 

Property: Spacious 3/2 waterfront pool home in Port Charlotte - Scenic Solitude (99424879)

Vrbo Listing Number: 9877226   

Expedia Group Health & Safety Case No: 153096069

Expedia Group has not yet received a response regarding potential health and safety concerns at this Vrbo property.

For reference, a Vrbo traveler who stayed at this property reported experiencing: 

" Pool was dirty and low on water. Baking dishes needed to be cleaned prior to use. Furniture pillows and mattress cover stained. Cobwebs, dust, exposed nails, holes and scuffed walls."
"Ants in the bathroom along with cockroaches of which six were caught in our actual overnight stay of 10 nights"

To help ensure the safety of future travelers, please submit your responses to the following questions regarding this property's health and safety features and practices by June 7th, 2026

Additionally, please include details for any actions already taken or plans to address the reported concerns: 

Is the pool water clear with the bottom of the pool visible (at its deepest point) at all times?
Is the pool(s) regularly cleaned (including the grease line)?
Is the water tested as per local regulations for pH balance and recorded in a logbook?
Is there a documented chemical disinfectant treatment system in place?
Are pool chemical dosing records retained?
The records should as a minimum be signed by the pool maintenance person and include date, times the chemicals
used and the dosage. Where more than one pool is a feature of the property, individual pool records must be
retained and be clearly labelled with the pool name. Is this the case?

Is there a process in place if the property is not up to guest standards?
If a cleaning company is used, how do they report to the owner/manager of issues found?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?
Do you have a cleaning schedule indicating which areas are cleaned, frequency of cleaning, chemicals to be used and any special protective equipment required?

Is a program of pest control measures in place?
Does this cover food control areas, public areas & bedrooms/apartments?
Please provide the name of your contracted pest control company
What is the date of the last inspection?
Did the Pest Control company find evidence of cockroach infestation?
If yes, what action was taken?
Were the surrounding rooms also treated? ¯
How often are rooms cleaned?
Is a thorough inspection of mattresses/furniture etc. carried out?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?

In the interest of safety, if we do not hear from you, we may be required to temporarily suspend this property on our sites until we receive your response.

Your professionalism and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. 

Best Regards,  

Raquel

Expedia Group Health & Safety Operations 

healthandsafety@expedia.com

tickets.subject: Gerbode Linda | Res #: 44407 | The Ref is running but not cooling.
tickets.description:
Hi team good day! Seeking your assistance on this matter. The guest reported that her refrigerator was not cooling, with the temperature rising inside, despite the lights being on and no unusual sounds or lights indicating a power issue.

They also confirmed that the refrigerator's interior lights are on, but the temperature has increased from 24 to 26 degrees in the freezer and from 46 to 50 degrees in the refrigerator section, indicating it is not functioning properly.

The refrigerator was tested by turning off and on the circuit breaker, which did not resolve the issue, suggesting a malfunction rather than a power supply problem and their are approximately $100 worth of food that was stored in the refrigerator, emphasizing the urgency of repair.

tickets.subject: Southern Comfort-elock
Hutchinson Mary And Charley | Unit: Signal Inn - Unit 06 | Res #: 17603 | Bad Review
tickets.description:
Hello Team, kindly assist on this email we received form Daniel RealRep with the email address Daniel Batcheler <danieldudleybatcheler@realrepboost.online>

As per the email: "Hello Signal Inn,

Does this review reflect your business?

"We have been staying at Signal Inn unit 6 since 2017, it has always been our home away from home. Unfortunately, we had a terrible experience, the unit was dirty, floors sandy and our feet were black walking around, the sheets were also filthy full of sand and dirt. I called the management company right away but they couldn't tell me when housekeeping would come. I washed the sheets and remade the bed. The worst was they entered the unit when we weren't there and my dog was there! It used to be such a great place when Julie, Lloyd, and Pam were there. I am heartbroken , I know the owners put alot of time and money into the place. I don't think we'll be coming back."

It was left by Mary Hutchinson and may fall under Google's removal guidelines.

When reviews breach guidelines, we take care of the filing and escalation.

No lock-ins or advance payments required.

Let me know if you'd like us to check it properly."
tickets.description_text: Hello Team, kindly assist on this email we received form Daniel RealRep with the email address Daniel Batcheler <danieldudleybatcheler@realrepboost.online>As per the email: "Hello Signal Inn,Does this review reflect your business?"We have been staying at Signal Inn unit 6 since 2017, it has always been our home away from home. Unfortunately, we had a terrible experience, the unit was dirty, floors sandy and our feet were black walking around, the sheets were also filthy full of sand and dirt. I called the management company right away but they couldn't tell me when housekeeping would come. I washed the sheets and remade the bed. The worst was they entered the unit when we weren't there and my dog was there! It used to be such a great place when Julie, Lloyd, and Pam were there. I am heartbroken , I know the owners put alot of time and money into the place. I don't think we'll be coming back."It was left by Mary Hutchinson and may fall under Google's removal guidelines.When reviews breach guidelines, we take care of the filing and escalation.No lock-ins or advance payments required.Let me know if you'd like us to check it properly."

tickets.subject: Unit: Harbour Happiness | Guest Name: Mehringer Steve | TV not working
tickets.description:
Hi Team,

Guest reported that the living room TV has not been functioning since check-in. The TV powers on but remains stuck on the apps screen. The remote control is unresponsive, and the guest is unable to navigate, change apps, or access any TV functions.

Troubleshooting Performed by Guest:
  • Tried using the remote control multiple times with no response.
  • Unplugged the TV from the wall and plugged it back in.
  • Issue persists after power cycling the TV.
  • Guest advised that the problem is present checked in.
Additional Notes:
Initial suspicion was that the remote batteries may be dead. He tried using other remotes, and interchange the batteries but issue still the same. For your assistance.

tickets.subject: Shade of Summer_cleaning
tickets.description:
Owner left early.  Can clean and inspect.  Side note all sheets have been replaced.  Owner note: 
We noted that there is a reservation in June. We have vacated the property. Sheets have all been purchased new, washed and beds are made so the cleaning staff are aware. Some of the sheets that were on the beds were not sheets we had purchased. Not sure where they came from. 

Towels are all clean and sorted. There are 16 full- size towels available for guests, some of which were recently purchased.  We can only guess that the towels mentioned were towels that were left by prior guests. We never disposed of them but we did during our recent stay. There were ample quality towels available for prior guests. 

Thanks,

Mike

tickets.subject: unit 2 signal inn
tickets.description: Hi Matt we had to have the AC company come out 2 times now They did not charge us for coming out again.. Before guest arrival the AC was leaking from the porch we vacuumed out the drains and the leaking stopped, the guest arrived, and after a bit called saying there was a leak inside from tech ceiling AC tech went out he said he unclogged a coupling that came apart and checked any other couplings to make sure they were glued he said we were all good. The next day all day we were good until around 6 pm last night the guest called again with the same issue. He came out again and said the drain line had a tear, the pitch on the drain line is causing cold water to get trapped and will be sending me a recommendation to move the drain line. Once i get the invoice ill send them to you if you need to see them, also we now need to repair the ceiling some patch work and painting.

Owner reported damage to the pool heater when in property.
Please include the following information in the claim ticket:   
  1. All related photos of the damages. (Do not need repaired photos, only damage photos)
  2. Reasoning for guest damage- what happened to the best of our knowledge? How did we determine guest damage vs wear and tear? Looks like guest damage based on the picture from owner and inspection
  3. Receipts for ALL damaged items- either repaired or replaced. 
  4.  The date of the damage occurred (to look up guest name).C Catron
  5. Property name Fishtail
We cannot submit a claim without the above items. Receipts and photos are of upmost importance to gain approval for the claim.

tickets.subject: Unit: Family Tides | Guest Name: Malagon Hector | Pest Infestation + Low Door Lock Battery Inspection
tickets.description:
Hi Team,

Guest reported multiple maintenance concerns requiring urgent attention:
  • Pest issue: Guest reported recurring ants and cockroaches inside the unit. They advised the pests have continued appearing despite cleaning, including a cockroach crawling on one guest while in the bathroom. Guest has provided photos/videos via email. Due to recent surgery, guest is highly concerned about sanitation and possible infection risk.
  • Door lock battery: Guest reported the electronic door lock appears to have low battery and expressed concern about being locked out again. 
Additional guest concerns noted (non-maintenance but for awareness):
  • Ongoing construction/window work causing disturbance
  • Confusion regarding assigned parking
  • Incorrect trash/recycling instructions provided at check-in
Guest has expressed health/safety concerns and request for compensation.

tickets.subject: Res. No. 43026 - Guest:  Joseph Hayes - A Wave From It All:     Issue:  access issues
tickets.description:
Hi Beth!

In Lucy's absence, I am not sure who to send this to, and I don't want to just ignore it.

Guest Joseph Hayes;  stay dates: May 16, 2026 - May 23, 2026 - noted the following issues:

Access issues as follows:

a)  Missing keys;
b)  Wrong/non-working access code;
c)  Missing unit number in instructions;
d)  Faulty key fob - (resolved on ticket 6466); and 
e)  Security/pre-registration issue (pre-registered daughter was not granted access to property)
 
The guest's review was as follows:

Our family’s experience at this property is poorly managed by the host and Gulf Coast Realty. I was given directions to go pick up the keys at the office at their lock box and no keys were there. I was also then given an access gate code to get in that went to the owner that never answered. Also, the check in instructions never had the unit number on it, I had to google it! The key fob provided did not work for access to the pool and the front gate, it took 4 days before I was able to get a replacement and only one set of keys for a family. Security would not let my pre-registered daughter into the property. The unit itself is fine however a towel and linen company should be provided, for sanitary reasons, hence bring your own towels.

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: VRBO Suspension Appeal - Scenic Solitude
tickets.description:
Dear Gulf Coast Vacation Rentals, 

Property: Spacious 3/2 waterfront pool home in Port Charlotte - Scenic Solitude (99424879)

Vrbo Listing Number: 9877226

Expedia Group Health & Safety Case No: 153096069

Expedia Group has been made aware of potential health and safety concerns at this Vrbo property.

For reference, a Vrbo traveler who stayed at this property reported experiencing: 

" Pool was dirty and low on water. Baking dishes needed to be cleaned prior to use. Furniture pillows and mattress cover stained. Cobwebs, dust, exposed nails, holes and scuffed walls."
"Ants in the bathroom along with cockroaches of which six were caught in our actual overnight stay of 10 nights"

To help ensure the safety of future travelers, please submit your responses to the following questions regarding this property's health and safety features and practices by May 31st, 2026 to avoid suspension.  

Additionally, please include details for any actions already taken or plans to address the reported concerns: 

Is the pool water clear with the bottom of the pool visible (at its deepest point) at all times?
Is the pool(s) regularly cleaned (including the grease line)?
Is the water tested as per local regulations for pH balance and recorded in a logbook?
Is there a documented chemical disinfectant treatment system in place?
Are pool chemical dosing records retained?
The records should as a minimum be signed by the pool maintenance person and include date, times the chemicals used and the dosage. Where more than one pool is a feature of the property, individual pool records must be retained and be clearly labelled with the pool name. Is this the case? 

Is there a process in place if the property is not up to guest standards?
If a cleaning company is used, how do they report to the owner/manager of issues found?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?
Do you have a cleaning schedule indicating which areas are cleaned, frequency of cleaning, chemicals to be used and any special protective equipment required?

Is a program of pest control measures in place?
Does this cover food control areas, public areas & bedrooms/apartments?
Please provide the name of your contracted pest control company
What is the date of the last inspection?
Did the Pest Control company find evidence of cockroach infestation?
If yes, what action was taken?
Were the surrounding rooms also treated? 
How often are rooms cleaned?
Is a thorough inspection of mattresses/furniture etc. carried out?
Are the results of these inspections recorded?

 

Your professionalism and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated.  

Best Regards,  

Raquel

Expedia Group Health & Safety Operations 

healthandsafety@expedia.com

Hi!  Your house is perfect for our long weekend surprise visit to our brother for his 75th birthday!  We grew up in Sarasota but live all over the place now!  We have never visited our brother's new house before and so we thought we would surprise him!  They literally live just to the east in Wild Oak Bay!!  Just the sisters, one niece and one granddaughter staying at the VRBO!

tickets.subject: PCA for My Great Escape Due Date: 6/27/2026
tickets.description:
1. Create ticket 1 month prior to due date and assign to account manager
2.Account Manager Schedules call with owner and notes ticket with date of call, assigns ticket to Project Manager
3. Project Manager completes PCA, creates scope of work and attaches to ticket, assigns ticket to Revenue Manager
4. Revenue Manager prepares financial report for home, attaches to ticket and assigns ticket to AM
5.  AM completes call with owner, notes ticket of results/feedback and closes ticket.

tickets.subject: Worthan RCc | Cancellation |  Res #: 53545
tickets.description:
Res #: 53545
Unit: Cypress Cove

Guest RC Warton has requested to cancel reservation #53545 after receiving an email from the homeowners association requesting personal information, which they are refusing to provide.

The guest has been advised that the required documents are for security, access control, and verification purposes. However, the guest remains firm on their decision to cancel and has requested that this be escalated to the team. They were also advised to contact VRBO directly, as their booking follows VRBO’s cancellation policy.

Since this is a long-term stay (58 nights),maybe we can still retain the reservation or further clarification can be provided to the guest regarding the HOA requirements to help avoid cancellation.


Thank you!

tickets.description:
Date and Time Reported:  May 22, 6pm
Date of Reservation:  CI: 05/22/2026 CO: 05/26/2026
Concerns: Property is dirty

1. Floor cleanliness (multiple areas)

  • Visible debris/small particles on the wooden floor in at least one area
  • Dirt/dust buildup noticeable in corners and along open floor space

2. Furniture / seating area

  • Chair and surrounding area appear generally used, but no deep cleaning issue is clearly visible
  • Possible minor dust accumulation around furniture edges

3. Rug / carpet areas

  • Patterned rugs show visible spots or dark debris
  • Some uneven cleanliness suggesting they may not have been vacuumed thoroughly

4. Bathroom / sink area

  • Sink appears to have minor residue or small debris present near the drain area
  • Could indicate incomplete wipe-down or finishing clean

5. Patio / outdoor pool area

  • Pool water shows visible debris floating (leaves/dirt)
  • Pool edge area appears to have scattered debris, suggesting it was not fully skimmed or cleaned before guest arrival

6. Bedding / linens

  • Bedding shows small visible stains or discoloration marks
  • May require replacement or deeper laundering check
Actions Taken: N.A
What's Needed:  Hi! Team, need assistance with this, please. Thank you! Guest is already discussing about refund or other alternatives (hotel or other property)

tickets.subject: Rikkola Kaitlyn/Beach House Residences Unit 1/  Res #: 51920/Love Bugs
tickets.description:
Date and Time Reported:  May 21, 4:13pm
Date of Reservation:  CI: 05/21/2026 - CO: 05/26/2026
Concerns: "There are love bugs everywhere in this unit."


Actions Taken: Informed guest  that love bugs are very common in Florida during this time of year and can quickly gather around doors, windows, and lights when they’re opened. I advised to  keep the doors and windows closed as much as possible to help prevent more from coming in.

What's Needed:  Guest is not happy about it, she said "We haven’t opened a single window or door. I have lots of pictures to support the ridiculous amount that are gathered. We have to do a full vacuum of the entire unit. It’s a little ridiculous to come to this amount. We understand they are common and come in but we just arrived… it should be clean."

tickets.subject: Res. No. 52756 - Guest:   Christine Ferguson - 7th Heaven      Issue:  front door lock not working
tickets.description:
Hi John! 

Guest Christine Ferguson stay dates: May 13 - May 16, 2026  - noted the following issue:

Front door lock not working.

The guest's review was as follows:

The kids and I had a great relaxing time and we definitely enjoyed our stay and will definitely stay here again.   We enjoyed the peaceful area.    Only thing i wasn’t thrilled about was when i did early check the front door lock wasn’t working so having to keep the kids chill after a 21hr drive was frustrating.

John, please could you have someone go out to check the door lock?

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Charlotte County Sales Tax removal of discretionary amount
tickets.description:
Charlotte County's discretionary tax amount of 1% falls off on 12/31/26, thereby the Charlotte County rate will decrease from 7% to 6%.  We will need to:

* notify account managers
* notify call center
* adjust existing 2027 reservations to adjust the sales/tourist tax amounts
* apply refunds if necessary for fully paid reservations
* let me know when this is completed

Revenue Driven Owner - Driving occupancy and revenue is the aim of the game. Happy to react to market conditions to drive occupancy.  A little of everything, looking for highest rate, property adorned with high end furniture, cutlery, decor etc.

-Pets? Open to discussion
-If previously rented, historic booking data... if available

Any other useful Info: waterfront - Boat lift, accessible to Myakka and Peace River

tickets.subject: scenic solitude-review dispute
tickets.description:
Owner is disputing the review the guest Tim Grossman left regarding bugs and animals on the roof.  We need to find out how we can either take the review down or provide a GC reply to this issue.  Owner note: 
I was at the house for a week and didn't see any bugs.
Zero, Bugs......
Not even an ant under the kitchen sink which is where I would see some if any

Did you have an exterminator visit the house before I arrived?

Either way, I contacted Truley Nolan and will have them come by next month to inspect and set up a contract............

These people were obviously hard up on money and looking for a discount
I saw the review they left, and it is ridicules and unfounded.

A dirty dish, nail holes were a shelf used to be in the ""LAUNDRY ROOM"" Really...
A mark on the wall where passed tenants banged the backs of the chairs against, is this the stuff that makes
a challenged human unable to live...

Regarding the 2 pics of the beetles, for all I know they were planted there, because "On my Life" 
I did not see a bug when I got there and the whole stay, I was there......No Bugs...

I didn't hear anything in the attic and did take a look.
There was a piece of wire mess that seemed to be moved by the roof over the foyer so I had that fixed...

Dave,
I want a rebuttal comment entered regarding their ridiculess claims.
I own a number of rentals throughout Florida and New York, I pride myself in maintaining them to the highest quality
possible.   Auburn Blvd is a very well kept and clean home, a 9 out of 10.  Anyone wanting to complain at least
complain about something that is legitimately disrupting their stay. 
Like, in the passed, I will have it remedied immediately.

People like these frustrate the crap out of me
Please let me know when the reply to their comment is posted.

Speak soon
Bill 516-250-9171  cell

tickets.subject: Res. No. 40861 - Tim Grossmann - Scenic Solitude: Issue: 6 of 6 - ants  & roaches in bathroom
tickets.description:
Hi Lucy 

Guest Tim Grossmann; stay dates: 25 April 2026 - 7 May 2026  - noted the following issues:

Issue 6 of 6 - ants  & roaches in bathroom

The guest's review was as follows:

This was our worst stay place in 36 years of yearly trips to SWF. Pool was dirty and low on water. Baking dishes needed to be cleaned prior to use. Furniture pillows and mattress cover stained. Cobwebs, dust, exposed nails, holes and scuffed walls. Three cases of something in the attic scratching and walking. Ants in the bathroom along with cockroaches of which six were caught in our actual overnight stay of 10 nights.

Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

tickets.subject: Res. No. 49531 - Guest  Erika Boyarski - Awake Zone     Issue:  Maintenance issues x 3
tickets.description:
Hi John!

Guest Erika Boyarski;  stay dates: May 7, 2026 - May 14, 2026 - she noted the following 3 x maintenance issues:

1.  Bed in the master bedroom squeaked and shook. 
2.  Please Put a bathroom lock on the door between the kitchen and guest bathroom door
3.  Fix the gaps in the dog fence.

The guest's review was as follows:

Actually very good plus., except the beds. My 70+yr old guests had the master bedroom and reported that it squeaked and shook. Please! Put a bathroom lock on the door between the kitchen and guest bathroom door🤪 The house was very clean The pool was the highlight! We were visiting during a very hot spell in Florida. Oh, please fix the gaps in the dog fence. We brought a small dog and had to be creative with some outdoor tables and a baby gate to close off the gaps. The garage was a nice plus for 1 of our vehicles.👍 Loved the tiled floors because they were easy to sweep up the sand. Lovely , quiet neighborhood where everyone waved as they drove by and I visited with other dog walkers in the neighborhood. Loved the tortoises.



Thank you!

Kind Regards,
Leeanne

Chelsea owner at Florida Dreams was in property. Informed GC there was an issue.  Here is her first email.  I will send her email from this weekend.  Does ARDI cover replacements on this type of issue?

Owner note: Wow, my place smelled like uri... by Dave Husak
Yesterday 1:52 PM
Dave Husak

Owner note: Wow, my place smelled like urine when I walked in the door. There's only 3 passes on the table instead of 4 and they are labeled with a $50  fee for replacement. This bedspread on master bed is UGLY and looks so drab. The couches have been cleaned and "restored to new condition" 2 times this year, but, you wouldn't know. I'll at least be replacing the pillows because they have visible dirt on them. There's a piece of baseboard that is gone leaving behind unsightly crumble appearance. In the second bedroom the blinds have a goo on them, like a lotion.

I am so disheartened by the condo, a place that I do love, but if I were a renter, I'd be underwhelmed. And the reviews can't handle any underwhelming  

I'm very disappointed in the services I'm paying for. 

Sarah Carstensen 

2nd note: Isn't there an insurance policy t... by Dave Husak
Yesterday 1:53 PM
Dave Husak

2nd note: Isn't there an insurance policy that guests are required to have when staying here? The twin beds in the second bedroom are urine saturated and despite using 3 full bottles and 2 partial bottles of products, manual scrubbing, the room smells strongly of urine. I also had mattress  protectors that were no longer in place. The amenity pass has not been addressed. How can we rectify this?

Sarah

  4.  The date of the damage occurred (to look up guest name).doyle Mueller april 17th
  5. Property name sandy pointe
  6. Johns billing notes: 
  7. We received guest feedback in April noting a cracked wall by the entryway. The guest requested confirmation that the damage was present prior to their arrival and asked not to be charged for the issue. We dispatched our technician to assess the concern. He determined that the dent in the lower part of the wall appeared to be caused by impact, such as kicking or striking with an object. He applied multiple coats of compound to repair the hole, repainted the area, and also installed a pivot hinge to fix the shower door. The issues have now been resolved.

    The report of our technician is attached to this email.
    The invoice total was $377.50
We cannot submit a claim without the above items. Receipts and photos are of upmost importance to gain approval for the claim.
